page: Hetzner Cloud ccx63 is a dedicated CPU server instance built on the x86_64 architecture. It features 48 vCPUs powered by AMD EPYC Milan processors running at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz, configured as 24 physical cores with 2 threads per core. The instance is equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Local storage is provided via a bundled 960 GB SSD, and the hypervisor layer is managed by QEMU.

In benchmark evaluations, the ccx63 demonstrates strong compute capabilities. Its single-core CPU performance via stress-ng ranks in the top tier (top 10%), while multi-core performance falls within the top 25%. Cryptographic and mathematical operations are accelerated by hardware support for AVX, AES, and SHA_NI instruction sets, reflected in strong Passmark CPU extended instructions and encryption scores. Memory bandwidth benchmarks for both cached and uncached reads and writes sit in the average tier, as do database operations and Redis SET throughput.

This hardware profile is appropriate for multi-threaded processing tasks, software compilation, and text decompression, where it achieves strong benchmark ratings. It also shows top-tier performance in text generation for small-scale language models. However, with memory bandwidth and database operations performing in the average range, it may not be the optimal choice for memory-bound high-performance computing or highly intensive database workloads. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent performance without resource contention, making it a reliable option for general-purpose application hosting, CI/CD pipelines, and moderate web serving.
description: Hetzner Cloud ccx63 is a dedicated CPU server featuring 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores powered by AMD EPYC Milan processors on the x86_64 architecture. Operating at 2.0 GHz, this instance includes 192.0 GB of RAM, establishing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-memory ratio, alongside a bundled 960 GB local SSD. Benchmark results place its single-core CPU performance in the top 10% and its multi-core performance in the top 25%, with strong results in software compilation and cryptographic execution. While memory bandwidth and database operations perform in the average tier, the dedicated CPU allocation ensures stable, predictable performance. This instance is recommended for multi-threaded workloads, CI/CD pipelines, software development environments, and general-purpose application hosting.
og_description: Hetzner Cloud ccx63 is a dedicated CPU server featuring 48 vCPUs on AMD EPYC Milan architecture, 192 GB RAM, and a 960 GB local SSD, delivering strong multi-core performance for general-purpose workloads.
meta_description: Hetzner Cloud ccx63 features 48 dedicated AMD EPYC Milan vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and 960 GB SSD storage for balanced, high-throughput compute workloads.
tagline: A dedicated AMD EPYC instance featuring forty-eight vCPUs and balanced memory for consistent, multi-threaded compute workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated vCPUs powered by AMD EPYC Milan processors
192 GB of system memory and 960 GB of local SSD storage
Top-tier single-core and strong multi-core CPU benchmark performance
Hardware-accelerated cryptography and vector calculations via AVX and AES
Optimized for software compilation, CI/CD, and multi-threaded applications
